frameworks.name: hidden Markov model
frameworks.one_line: States parameterized as PCA decompositions to capture time-varying FC

frameworks.name: principal component analysis
frameworks.one_line: Two-step PCA pre-reduction shown to bias FC state estimation

frameworks.name: Gaussian mixture model
frameworks.one_line: Conventional mixture of Gaussians on PCA scores used as baseline

frameworks.name: time-varying functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: Spontaneous between-region covariance patterns interpreted as dynamic states

core_question: Can time-varying functional connectivity in fMRI be estimated reliably without two-step PCA-then-HMM biases?
key_assumptions: State-specific covariance structures capture distinct FC patterns
Data are high-dimensional with few time points
manipulations: Replaced PCA-then-HMM pipeline with joint HMM-PCA estimation
Compared against PCA + HMM and PCA + Gaussian mixture baselines
key_findings: Two-step PCA suffers systematic biases and loss of sensitivity for FC state recovery
Joint HMM-PCA recovers distinct FC patterns and switching dynamics
HMM-PCA outperforms PCA-based alternatives empirically and theoretically
interpretations: Fusing dimensionality reduction with FC state estimation in one step avoids two-step pitfalls
State-specific PCA decompositions provide a flexible description of time-varying FC
